competitors.name: AnythingLLM
competitors.keyDifference: General-purpose LLM chat with RAG; AiDoc.WebNew is a purpose-built RAG admin portal with MCP endpoints and ChromaDB-specific management
competitors.notes: Full-stack open-source RAG app with multi-user Docker deployment, supports LanceDB/Qdrant/ChromaDB, role-based access control, workspace management

competitors.name: Vectara
competitors.keyDifference: Managed RAG-as-a-service with proprietary tech; AiDoc.WebNew gives direct ChromaDB control with MCP integration
competitors.notes: Enterprise-grade RAG platform with Admin Center for on-premise deployments, system health monitoring, tenant management, corpora management

competitors.name: Vector Admin
competitors.keyDifference: Pure database admin tool without RAG pipeline management or MCP integration; AiDoc.WebNew combines admin with semantic search and MCP endpoints
competitors.notes: Universal vector database management UI supporting Pinecone, Chroma, Qdrant, Weaviate. 2.2k GitHub stars. Browse, search, manage collections

competitors.name: Strative Connect
competitors.keyDifference: Commercial enterprise platform for large-scale RAG deployments; AiDoc.WebNew is self-hosted for internal teams with MCP-native integration
competitors.notes: Plug-and-play enterprise RAG management platform, configurable vector databases, indexing methods, retrieval algorithms, deploys within customer VPC

competitors.name: LanceDB Cloud
competitors.keyDifference: Vector database with its own storage format, not a management portal for ChromaDB; AiDoc.WebNew manages ChromaDB and exposes MCP endpoints
competitors.notes: Multimodal vector database for RAG and hybrid search at petabyte scale. SOC2 Type II, GDPR, HIPAA compliance. Managed cloud offering

competitors.name: Chroma (with Context-1)
competitors.keyDifference: Chroma is the underlying database; AiDoc.WebNew adds the web management UI, semantic search interface, and MCP endpoint layer
competitors.notes: Open-source embedding database. Released Context-1 (20B parameter agentic search model) in 2026. SDK-only, no built-in web admin portal
